name: Sierra de Enguera and Pico Caroche Loop
name_original: La Ruta del Agua por la Canal de Navarrés — Circular desde Enguera
description: This difficult 49.7-mile mountain biking route from Enguera explores the Canal de Navarrés, featuring waterfalls and diverse terrain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Enguera offers diverse natural landscapes and challenging terrain. The region is characterized by the Sierra de Enguera mountain range, featuring peaks over 1,000 meters and rich Mediterranean forests of pines and holm oaks. Deep ravines like Barranco de la Hoz and Barranco del Gatillo crisscross the area, providing varied elevation and scenic backdrops for mountain bike trails. The inland mountains of Valencia, where Enguera is located, offer a mix of natural trails suitable for XC, trail, and enduro riding. Best mountain bike trails around Enguera The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Enguera and Pico Caroche Loop, a difficult 49.7 miles (80.0 km) trail that takes 7 hours 17 minutes to complete. This route explores the prominent Sierra de Enguera mountain range, offering significant climbs and descents. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Palace of the Counts of Cirat – Castle of Almansa loop from Camping Los Carasoles, a moderate 31.0 miles (49.9 km) path. This route features varied terrain through the Valencian countryside, passing historical landmarks. Local mountain bikers also love the Fuente las Arenas loop from Camping Los Carasoles, a 19.3 miles (31.1 km) trail leading through Mediterranean forest, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Mountain biking around Enguera is defined by the Sierra de Enguera mountain range, deep ravines, and extensive Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for all ability levels, from beginner-friendly paths to technical sections with steep, rocky descents. The routes in Enguera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Enguera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Enguera?
A: Enguera offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 50 routes available. These range from easy paths to very challenging rides, catering to all skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Enguera?
A: The terrain around Enguera is incredibly varied, characterized by the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Enguera&lt;/strong&gt; mountain range with peaks over 1,000 meters, deep ravines like &lt;em&gt;Barranco de la Hoz&lt;/em&gt;, and extensive Mediterranean forests. You'll find a mix of natural trails suitable for XC, trail, and enduro riding, from beginner-friendly paths to technical sections with steep, loose, and rocky descents.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Enguera?
A: Yes, Enguera has several mountain bike trails suitable for beginners. Out of the 50 available routes, 4 are classified as easy, offering a great introduction to the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Enguera?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Enguera offers 13 difficult mountain bike trails. One notable route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e909613766/sierra-de-enguera-and-pico-caroche-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Enguera and Pico Caroche Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 80 km trail with significant climbs and descents through the prominent mountain range.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that form a loop in Enguera?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Enguera are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente las Arenas loop from Camping Los Carasoles&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457549" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palace of the Counts of Cirat – Castle of Almansa loop from Camping Los Carasoles&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see while mountain biking in Enguera?
A: Enguera's trails offer stunning natural features. You can explore deep ravines like &lt;em&gt;Barranco de la Hoz&lt;/em&gt; and &lt;em&gt;Barranco del Gatillo&lt;/em&gt;, which feature dramatic rock walls and spectacular landscapes. The region is also known for beautiful water features, including the &lt;em&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera&lt;/em&gt; near Anna with its 25-meter waterfall, and the lush Mediterranean forests of the Sierra de Enguera.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles accessible by mountain bike near Enguera?
A: Yes, the region around Enguera is rich in historical landmarks. You can find several castles and historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457549" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palace of the Counts of Cirat – Castle of Almansa loop from Camping Los Carasoles&lt;/a&gt; passes by historical landmarks. Other notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4042648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Encomienda de Enguera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Vallada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Enguera?
A: The mountain bike trails in Enguera are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Many riders praise the amazing variety of natural trails, the stunning scenery, and the options for all skill levels, from challenging climbs to thrilling descents through Mediterranean forests.

Q: Is there public transport access to mountain bike trailheads in Enguera?
A: While Enguera is well-suited for mountain biking, direct public transport access to all trailheads can be limited. It's often recommended to plan your route and consider local transport options or personal vehicle use to reach specific starting points.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Enguera?
A: Many routes, especially those starting from camping sites like Camping Los Carasoles, offer convenient parking facilities. For other trailheads, parking can often be found in or near the town of Enguera, or in designated areas close to popular starting points. Always check local signage for parking regulations.

Q: Are permits required for mountain biking in the Sierra de Enguera region?
A: Generally, permits are not required for mountain biking on established trails in the Sierra de Enguera. However, it's always advisable to respect local regulations, especially within natural reserves like the Umbría-La Plana Municipal Nature Reserve, and to stay on marked paths to protect the environment.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Enguera?
A: Many natural trails in Enguera are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the ride with your canine companion. However, it's crucial to keep your dog under control, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to adhere to any specific rules posted in natural parks or reserves.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Enguera?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Enguera is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winter offers cooler temperatures but can have unpredictable conditions.
tips: Site with beautiful views and very quiet. It is reached by paths in good condition with some stretch of well-settled rock.
Within La Canal de Navarrés you will find this very peculiar site known by the locals. An old town located in a remote area that gives it a particular charm.
Forest track in very good condition for the most part with a firm surface of compacted gravel. Along the way you will have incredible views of the Caroche massif and La Juanera.

Very nice site with recreation area, parking and camping. No mobile phone service. Los Botijos is a bar that must be booked 24 hours in advance. From here you can start a section of the water route visiting very showy puddles and waterfalls and during the summer it is ideal to cool off with a swim!
Incredible place with waterfall and puddle with the possibility of bathing. Very quiet surrounded by a very pleasant environment. It can be reached both by bike and on foot.

faqs.question: What level of fitness and skill is required for this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging mountain bike ride, consistently rated as 'difficult'. It demands very good fitness and advanced riding skills. You should be prepared for some technical sections where you might need to push your bike. The technical difficulty is estimated to be between STS S3 and S6 on the Single Track Scale.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is a varied mix of surfaces, including forest tracks, dirt roads, and asphalted sections. While not considered 'extremely technical' overall, it features significant elevation changes and some technical path or singletrack sections. A robust and well-equipped mountain or gravel bike with good tire width is recommended.

faqs.question: What natural attractions and landmarks can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: As its name suggests, the route highlights aquatic elements and natural beauty. You'll encounter natural landscapes, waterfalls, and charming traditional towns. Notable features include the Charco del Paso, and you'll pass near the Cabrentá, Barranco de las Ventanas, and the Chorradores. The route also offers spectacular views throughout.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Comunitat Valenciana. Fredes - el Pinós trail, Barranco del Gatillo, Pico del Fraile, Rio Grande - Abrigo de Voro, Barranco de la Carrasca, and Camino de la Vereda.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The most favorable seasons for riding are spring and autumn. During these times, the climate is mild, and trail conditions are typically optimal, making for a more enjoyable experience.

faqs.question: Is the Water Route through the Canal de Navarrés dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route are not detailed, trails in natural areas like the Sierra de Enguera generally require dogs to be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice responsible pet ownership.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail or the Sierra de Enguera?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this mountain biking route or the Sierra de Enguera natural area. However, always respect local regulations and private property signs you may encounter along the way.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead in Enguera?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting from Enguera, you can typically find parking within the town itself. Look for public parking areas or designated spots suitable for trail access. Specific trailhead parking details are not provided, but Enguera serves as the primary starting and ending point.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to rest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route allows for stops in different towns, offering opportunities for amenities. You might also find facilities like the Recreational area and campsite of Benefetal "Los Botijos". For more rustic stops, there are huts such as Casa La Venta (Cañada Negra) and Casas de Benalí.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied and challenging terrain, a robust and well-equipped mountain bike or gravel bike is highly recommended. Ensure your bike has good tire width and sufficient sprockets to handle the significant elevation changes and technical sections.

faqs.question: Can I find accommodation or places to eat in Enguera or nearby towns?
faqs.answer: Yes, Enguera and the towns along the Canal de Navarrés region offer various amenities. You can find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in Enguera and other traditional towns you pass through, making it possible for overnight stays or resupply during a bikepacking trip.
